DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF EVA ARMADA, ANTONIO SALSEDO, RUDY CONCEPCION, LORNA BINALEY'S MOTION TO FURTHER COMPEL DEFENDANTS' DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES. GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 2.1, 2.2, 12.1, AND 15.1 WITH VERIFIED AMENDED ANSWERS BEING DUE BY PERSONAL SERVICE ON JULY 19. DENIED AS TO 17. DEFENDANTS DON'T OPPOSE AS TO 2.1 AND 2.2. AS TO 12.1 AND 15.1, CCP REQUIRES THE MOTION TO BE SERVED WITHIN 45 DAYS OF THE VERIFIED RESPONSE. IT IS TRUE THAT NO VERIFICATION WAS REQUIRED FOR THE RESPONSES TO 12.1 AND 15.1 SINCE THEY WERE JUST OBJECTIONS. BUT THE CCP SETS NO DEADLINE TO MOVE TO COMPEL AS TO UNVERIFIED RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO MEET AND CONFER WITH DEFENDANTS ABOUT SCHEDULING THE DATE OF HEARING OF THE MOTION IN VIOLATION OF THE LOCAL RULES, ALTHOUGH IMPROPER, IS NOT GROUNDS FOR DENYING THE MOTION. DEFENDANTS APPARENTLY DON'T HAVE A PROBLEM WITH THE DATE SINCE THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THEY SOUGHT TO CHANGE IT. WHETHER OR NOT THE LAWSUIT IS TECHNICALLY PROPER IS NOT AN ISSUE FOR THIS MOTION. THE COURT SET A TRIAL DATE IN AUGUST, SO DISCOVERY MUST PROCEED UNTIL THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF, OR THE CASE IS DISMISSED, WHICHEVER IS FIRST. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO INTERROGATORY 17. THE "YES" ANSWER IS PROPER UNLESS DEFENDANTS DID NOT ADMIT ANY OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE IN THE PAPERS THAT DEFENDANTS DID NOT ADMIT ANY OF THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION. SANCTIONS OF $1,500 ARE AWARDED. THE FAILURE TO ANSWER 2.1 AND 2.2, AND THE OBJECTIONS TO 12.1 AND 15.1 ARE WITHOUT JUSTIFICATION. DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY BY EMAIL TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT BRUCE.HIGHMAN@HIGHMANLAW.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of And Motion To Further Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions And Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Jul-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF EVA ARMADA, ANTONIO SALSEDO, RUDY CONCEPCION, LORNA BINALEY's Motion To Further Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions And Attorney'S Fees.

Granted as to form interrogatories 2.1, 2.2, 12.1, and 15.1 with verified amended answers being due by personal service on July 19. Denied as to 17. Defendants don't oppose as to 2.1 and 2.2. As to 12.1 and 15.1, CCP requires the motion to be served within 45 days of the verified response. It is true that no verification was required for the responses to 12.1 and 15.1 since they were just objections. But the CCP sets no deadline to move to compel as to unverified responses. Plaintiff's failure to meet and confer with defendants about scheduling the date of hearing of the motion in violation of the local rules, although improper, is not grounds for denying the motion. Defendants apparently don't have a problem with the date since there is no evidence they sought to change it.

Whether or not the lawsuit is technically proper is not an issue for this motion. The court set a trial date in August, so discovery must proceed until the discovery cutoff, or the case is dismissed, whichever is first.

The motion is denied as to interrogatory 17. The "Yes" answer is proper unless defendants did not admit any of the requests for admission. There is no evidence in the papers that defendants did not admit any of the requests for admission.

Sanctions of $1,500 are awarded. The failure to answer 2.1 and 2.2, and the objections to 12.1 and 15.1 are without justification.

Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly by email to the Judge Pro Tem at
bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
